title: Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary (Marathi: कारंजा सोहोल वन्यजीव अभयारण्य) is a protected area in the Karanja talukas of Washim district in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary (Marathi: कारंजा सोहोल वन्यजीव अभयारण्य) is a protected area in the Karanja talukas of Washim district in Maharashtra, India. It was created in 2000 to preserve the black buck population. It covers 1,832 ha (18.32 km2) of forest and grasslands.

topPlaces.name: Adan Dam
topPlaces.description: Adan Dam, is an earthfill and rockfill dam on Aran river near Karanja, Washim district in state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Karanja Lad
topPlaces.description: Karanja Lad, or Karanja, is a city of a Municipal council in Washim district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The town is named after Saint Karanj.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Nanded Airport (140 km) and the nearest railway station is Nagpur Junction (183 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Karanja Sohol Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adan Dam, Karanja Lad.
